name: Webhook Integration
description: External webhook integration with signature verification, event handling, and database sync. Follows security-first approach.

steps.name: Research webhook documentation
steps.action_template: Spike: Research {{service_name}} webhook API and security requirements
steps.input_template: Service documentation URL or API reference
steps.output_template: Spike report with endpoint requirements, event types, signature format, and security requirements

steps.name: Create webhook types
steps.action_template: Create TypeScript types for {{service_name}} webhook payloads

steps.name: Create webhook route handler
steps.output_template: Route handler at {{api_path}}/webhooks/{{service_name}}/route.ts with verification and event routing

steps.name: Create event handlers
steps.input_template: Route handler dispatches to event handlers

steps.name: Create database sync logic
steps.action_template: Create functions to sync {{service_name}} data to database
steps.output_template: Sync functions that update local database from webhook events

steps.name: Add webhook configuration
steps.action_template: Add {{service_name}} webhook secret to environment config
steps.output_template: Environment variable {{SERVICE_NAME}}_WEBHOOK_SECRET added to .env.example and documentation

steps.name: Add integration tests
steps.action_template: Create integration tests for webhook handler

steps.name: Document webhook setup
steps.output_template: Documentation updated with webhook URL, required secrets, and event handling

adaptation_notes: Step 1 (spike) is CRITICAL - do not skip for unfamiliar services
Signature verification algorithm varies by service - check documentation
Consider adding webhook event logging for debugging production issues
